title: pomaderris commutata
intro: Pomaderris commutata, also known as the coastal pomaderris, is a small shrub native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, and its health benefits may include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. Pomaderris commutata can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
